title: Sensible Defaults
concept: Supplying default values for omitted arguments so the tool execution doesn't crash the loop.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, what happens when a tool call fails schema validation?

options.text: The error is sent back to the model so it can fix its arguments and retry
The whole agent shuts down forever
The tool silently returns fake data

exercises.explain: Feedback loops send the validation error back to the model as a message. The model reads what went wrong and retries with corrected, valid arguments instead of giving up.

cards.title: The Missing Argument
cards.body: An agent calls a weather tool but forgets to say which city. Without a plan, the tool crashes and the loop breaks. What if the tool just picked a smart fallback instead?

cards.title: What Is a Sensible Default?
cards.body: A default is a backup value your tool uses when the model leaves an argument out. It keeps the tool running instead of crashing when something is missing.

cards.title: Coffee Shop Defaults
cards.body: If you order coffee but don't say the size, the barista makes a medium. They don't stop and refuse to serve you. Defaults let your tool 'serve' even when a detail is missing.

cards.title: Defaults in Action
cards.body: A search tool has a 'max_results' argument. If the model omits it, the tool uses 10 instead of crashing. The search still works and the loop keeps going smoothly.

cards.title: Myth: Defaults Fix Everything
cards.body: Not true! Defaults only help for optional details. If a truly required value like 'city' is missing, a default might give wrong results, so some arguments still need real input.

cards.title: Crash vs. Default
cards.body: See what happens to the loop when an argument is left out.

left.text: Missing 'size' argument makes the tool crash and stops the agent.

right.text: Missing 'size' becomes 'medium' and the tool runs fine.

exercises.prompt: Why do tools use sensible defaults for omitted arguments?

options.text: So the tool can still run instead of crashing the agent's loop
To make the model type more arguments
To hide errors from the user permanently
To slow the agent down on purpose

exercises.explain: Defaults fill in missing optional values so the tool executes normally. This keeps the agent's loop moving instead of breaking on a missing detail.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A sensible default should be used for a truly required value like a user's exact destination city.
exercises.explain: Defaults are great for optional details, but guessing a required value like a destination could give wrong results. Required values still need real input from the model.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about defaults.
exercises.sentence: If the model omits an optional argument, the tool uses a default value so it does not crash.
exercises.explain: A default value is the planned backup the tool falls back to when an optional argument is left out, keeping the loop alive.

exercises.prompt: Which tool description was written by an AI to explain sensible defaults?

options.text: The 'sort_order' argument defaults to 'newest' when the model does not specify one, so results always return in a predictable order.
sort thing maybe idk it breaks sometimes lol

exercises.explain: The first option is clear, complete, and explains exactly what the default is and why. The others are vague or unfinished, unlike a polished AI explanation.

exercises.prompt: An agent calls a 'list_files' tool but omits the optional 'folder' argument. The tool is designed with a sensible default. What should happen, and why?

options.text: The tool uses the default folder (like the home directory) and runs, keeping the loop alive
The tool crashes and ends the agent session
The tool waits forever until a human types the folder
The tool deletes all files to be safe

exercises.explain: A sensible default replaces the missing optional 'folder' with a reasonable fallback, so the tool executes and the agent keeps working. Defaults prevent crashes on optional arguments, but remember they should never be used to guess truly required values.
